summary: Add Elevation Profile Data to Trail Response
description:
Add elevation gain, elevation loss, and elevation profile array to the GET /trails/{id} response.

Currently the trail response only includes basic metadata (name, difficulty, distance). Guests and the mobile app need elevation data to properly display trail profiles and help guests plan their hikes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- GET /trails/{id} includes elevationGainMeters (number)
- GET /trails/{id} includes elevationLossMeters (number)
- GET /trails/{id} includes elevationProfile (array of {distanceKm, elevationMeters} points)
- Elevation data is sourced from the trail-geo-data service
- Response time does not degrade beyond current p95 (200ms)

summary: Adventure Category Classification for Check-In UI Patterns
description:
Implement adventure category classification to drive different check-in UI patterns based on adventure type.

The check-in service currently uses a single UI flow for all adventure types. Different categories (guided tours, self-service trails, equipment-required activities) need distinct check-in patterns:
- Guided tours: group assembly, guide assignment, safety briefing confirmation
- Self-service trails: wristband scan, waiver confirmation, trail map distribution
- Equipment activities: equipment checkout, fitting verification, safety gear check

The adventure catalog service should expose a 'category' field that the check-in UI consumes to determine which flow to render.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Adventure catalog API returns category enum: GUIDED_TOUR, SELF_SERVICE, EQUIPMENT_REQUIRED
- Check-in service reads category and selects appropriate UI pattern
- Fallback to SELF_SERVICE if category is missing or unknown
- Category classification rules are configurable without code deployment

Enable unregistered guests (walk-ups without prior reservation) to self-service check-in for adventures.

Currently, the check-in flow requires a valid reservationId. Walk-up guests without reservations cannot use the self-service kiosks and must go to a staffed counter, creating long wait times during peak hours.

Proposed Flow:
1. Guest approaches kiosk without reservation
2. System creates a temporary guest identity (via svc-guest-identity)
3. Guest selects available adventure and time slot (via svc-scheduling-orchestrator)
4. System creates a just-in-time reservation
5. Normal check-in flow proceeds with the new reservation

Acceptance Criteria:
- Self-service kiosk supports 'walk-up' mode without reservationId
- Temporary guest identity is created with minimal PII (name, photo for safety)
- Available adventures are shown based on real-time capacity
- Just-in-time reservation is created and linked to check-in
- If guest later links to a registered profile, temporary identity merges
- All walk-up check-ins are flagged for capacity analytics

summary: Guide Schedule Overwrite Bug in Scheduling Orchestrator
description:
The scheduling orchestrator is overwriting guide enrichment data when processing concurrent schedule updates.

Reported Issue:
When two schedule updates arrive within a short window for the same guide, the second update overwrites the enrichment data (certifications, language skills, specializations) attached by the first update. This causes guides to appear unqualified for adventures they are certified for.

Impact:
- Guides are being removed from adventure assignments they are qualified for
- Manual re-assignment is required, causing operational delays
- Approximately 15-20 incorrect removals per day during peak scheduling periods

Suspected Root Cause:
The guide enrichment step in the scheduling pipeline does not use optimistic locking or version checks. The PUT /guides/{id}/schedule endpoint overwrites the entire schedule object instead of merging changes.

Evidence:
- Production logs show 'Guide enrichment data overwritten' warnings
- Elastic logs show concurrent PUT requests to the same guide within 50ms windows

summary: Add Wristband RFID Field to Check-In Record
description:
Add an optional wristbandRfid field to the check-in record schema to support RFID wristband tracking.

The park is rolling out RFID-enabled wristbands for adventure tracking and emergency location services. The check-in service needs to capture the wristband RFID tag ID during the check-in process so that guest location can be tracked throughout the adventure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- POST /check-ins accepts optional wristbandRfid field (string, max 64 chars)
- GET /check-ins/{id} returns wristbandRfid if present
- RFID field is validated against a regex pattern: ^[A-F0-9]{8,16}$
- Duplicate RFID values are rejected (uniqueness constraint per active check-in)
- RFID field is included in the check-in event published to the event bus
- Field is nullable for adventures that don't use wristband tracking
